tasks.title: Implement Secure Secret Management for S3/R2 Credentials
tasks.description: Replace the standard Kubernetes Secret for S3/R2 credentials with a secure secret management solution to prevent plaintext credentials in version control and enable secret rotation.
subtasks: Research and select a suitable secure secret management solution (e.g., External Secrets Operator, HashiCorp Vault, Sealed Secrets).
Deploy the chosen secret management solution to the cluster.
Migrate the sigma1-s3-r2-credentials secret to use the new solution.
Update any deployment manifests that reference the secret to use the new mechanism.
Define a secret rotation policy and mechanism for the S3/R2 credentials.
Remove the original plaintext Kubernetes Secret manifest from version control.
Test secret access from a test application.
acceptance_criteria: S3/R2 credentials are no longer stored as plaintext Kubernetes Secrets in version control.
A secure secret management solution is deployed and operational.
Applications can successfully retrieve and use S3/R2 credentials via the new solution.
A secret rotation mechanism is in place and documented.
No kubectl get secret sigma1-s3-r2-credentials -n sigma1 -o yaml reveals plaintext credentials.

tasks.title: Enforce Internal TLS for PostgreSQL and Redis Connections
tasks.description: Modify the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap and database configurations to explicitly enforce TLS for all internal pod-to-database communication for both PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey.
subtasks: Update the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap to use sslmode=require for PostgreSQL and rediss:// for Redis/Valkey connection strings.
Verify that the CloudNative-PG operator and Redis/Valkey operator are configured to serve connections over TLS.
Ensure necessary certificates are provisioned and trusted within the cluster for internal TLS.
Test connectivity from a test application pod using the updated connection strings.
acceptance_criteria: The sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap contains connection strings enforcing TLS for PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey.
PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey instances only accept TLS-encrypted connections.
Applications can successfully connect to PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey using TLS-enforced connection strings.
Network traffic between application pods and database pods is encrypted.

tasks.title: Implement Pod Security Standards for Operators and Databases
tasks.description: Apply restricted Pod Security Standards (PSS) to the databases and sigma1 namespaces and configure securityContexts for all operator and database deployments to run as non-root, drop unnecessary capabilities, and use read-only root filesystems where applicable.
subtasks: Apply a Pod Security Admission (PSA) policy to the databases and sigma1 namespaces to enforce the restricted profile.

Modify the deployment manifests for CloudNative-PG operator (Task 3), Redis/Valkey operator (Task 4), sigma1-postgres cluster (Task 5), and sigma1-valkey instance (Task 6) to include securityContext configurations.
Ensure securityContext settings include runAsNonRoot: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, capabilities: { drop: ["ALL"] }, and readOnlyRootFilesystem: true where appropriate.
Redeploy the affected operators and database instances.
Verify that pods are running with the specified security contexts.
acceptance_criteria: Pod Security Admission is enforced for databases and sigma1 namespaces.
All pods for CloudNative-PG operator, Redis/Valkey operator, sigma1-postgres, and sigma1-valkey are running with runAsNonRoot: true.
Pods do not have unnecessary capabilities.
Pods are running with read-only root filesystems where applicable.
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <namespace> shows the applied securityContext settings.

tasks.title: Deploy Centralized Security Logging and Database Audit Logging
tasks.description: Implement a centralized logging solution for the Kubernetes cluster and enable audit logging for the sigma1-postgres database to capture security-relevant events and sensitive data access.
subtasks: Select and deploy a centralized logging stack (e.g., Fluentd/Fluent Bit + Elasticsearch/Loki + Grafana).
Configure the logging agents to collect logs from all relevant pods in databases and sigma1 namespaces.
Enable the pgAudit extension for the sigma1-postgres cluster.
Configure pgAudit to log security-relevant events (e.g., DDL, DML, role changes, failed logins).
Ensure PostgreSQL audit logs are collected by the centralized logging solution.
Create initial dashboards or alerts for critical security events in the logging platform.
acceptance_criteria: A centralized logging solution is deployed and collecting logs from cluster components.
pgAudit extension is enabled and configured for sigma1-postgres.
PostgreSQL audit logs are visible and searchable in the centralized logging platform.
Security-relevant events (e.g., DDL operations, failed login attempts) are logged and accessible.
Basic security dashboards or alerts are configured.
